productive_queries: frontiers oncology insta360 X3 radiation therapy virtual tour 2025
"insta360 X3" OR "insta360 X4" clinical study hospital 2025
confirmed_exclusions_this_run: Telmo dos Reis — already featured on Insta360 enterprise blog ('5 Tips for Filming a VR Nature Documentary in the African Savannah')
Mission Taroni / Dorothy Project — linked to Mathieu Baptista who is on exclusion list
key_learnings: Week A (Reddit + YouTube) searches via standard web search are inherently ineffective — Reddit posts and YouTube pages do not surface through site: operator searches
Future Week A runs should try old.reddit.com/r/insta360 JSON API directly rather than Google site: operator
Academic journal methods sections remain the most reliable source for specific camera model citations

Healthcare and clinical settings are a significantly undertapped Insta360 use case domain — two RCT-confirmed stories found across Week B/C/A runs (Kukharuk VR therapy, Schulz radiation oncology virtual tour)
Academic paper pattern: search by specific camera model + application domain + year + journal name gives better results than generic searches
403 on WebFetch from academic journals (Frontiers, PMC, Nature) is bot-detection, not a paywall — stories are still valid but require human verification of source quote accuracy
Never include based on inference — must find explicit 'Insta360' brand mention before including any story
